mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY growth in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY decline in industrial production signals a contraction in supply capacity that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a supply-side boom.

mechanism_narrative: A 50-bps increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% MoM increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that boosts jobless benefits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a labor market boom.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY growth in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY decline in housing starts signals a contraction in supply capacity that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a supply-side boom.

mechanism_narrative: A 25-bps decrease in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that lowers discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% MoM decline in initial unemployment claims signals a strengthening labor market that boosts job creation and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the face of a labor market downturn.
